prompt: Who holds the NBA record for most career rebounds?
explanation: Wilt Chamberlain holds the all-time rebounding record with over 23,900 career rebounds.

prompt: Who holds the NBA record for most career assists?
explanation: John Stockton's 15,806 career assists is an NBA record by a wide margin.

prompt: Who holds the NBA record for most career steals?
explanation: John Stockton also holds the career steals record, alongside his assists record.

prompt: Who holds the NBA record for most career blocked shots?
explanation: Hakeem Olajuwon holds the all-time blocks record with 3,830 career blocks.

prompt: Which player holds the record for most consecutive games played in NBA history, nicknamed 'Iron Man'?
explanation: A.C. Green played 1,192 consecutive games, an NBA record.

prompt: Which team won an NBA-record 33 consecutive games during the 1971-72 season?
explanation: The Lakers' 33-game winning streak in 1971-72 remains an NBA record.

prompt: Which player anchored the Lakers' record 33-game winning streak in 1971-72?
explanation: Wilt Chamberlain and Jerry West led the Lakers to their historic win streak that season.

prompt: Which player scored a rookie-record 23,000+ career points but is best known for his rivalry with Larry Bird?
explanation: Magic Johnson's rivalry with Larry Bird defined the NBA's resurgence in the 1980s.

prompt: Which two college teams did Magic Johnson and Larry Bird famously face off in the 1979 NCAA championship game before their pro rivalry?
explanation: Magic's Michigan State beat Bird's Indiana State in the 1979 NCAA title game, a launching point for their rivalry.

prompt: How many free throws did Wilt Chamberlain make during his legendary 100-point game in 1962?
explanation: Chamberlain, usually a poor free-throw shooter, made 28 of 32 free throws during his 100-point game.

prompt: Which team won the first-ever NBA championship in 1947 (as the BAA)?
explanation: The Philadelphia Warriors won the first BAA championship in 1947, a precursor league to the NBA.

prompt: In what year did the Basketball Association of America (BAA) merge with the National Basketball League (NBL) to form the modern NBA?
explanation: The BAA and NBL merged in 1949 to form the NBA as it exists today.

prompt: Which team has won a record 11 championships under a single head coach, Red Auerbach's Celtics?
explanation: Red Auerbach coached the Celtics to nine titles as head coach, and served as executive for further championships, totaling a franchise record.

prompt: Which player won a record 11 NBA championships as a player, all with the Boston Celtics?
explanation: Bill Russell won 11 championships in 13 seasons with the Celtics, an unmatched record.

prompt: Which center is Bill Russell most famously associated with rivaling throughout the 1960s?
explanation: The Russell-Chamberlain rivalry was one of the defining storylines of 1960s NBA basketball.

prompt: Which player's dominant 1961-62 season saw him average over 50 points per game, a record that still stands?
explanation: Chamberlain averaged 50.4 points per game in the 1961-62 season, an unmatched record.

prompt: Which player famously averaged a triple-double for an entire NBA season in 1961-62?
explanation: Oscar Robertson averaged a triple-double for the entire 1961-62 season, a feat unmatched for decades.

prompt: Who was the second player, after Oscar Robertson, to average a triple-double for a full NBA season, doing so in 2017?
explanation: Russell Westbrook averaged a triple-double for the 2016-17 season, matching Robertson's historic feat decades later.

prompt: Which team's 1980s dynasty featured the 'Big Three' of Larry Bird, Kevin McHale, and Robert Parish?
explanation: Bird, McHale and Parish anchored the Celtics' championship teams of the 1980s.

prompt: Which award did Wes Unseld win in the same season, an extremely rare double achievement in 1968-69?
options: Rookie of the Year and MVP in the same season
Sixth Man and Rookie of the Year
explanation: Wes Unseld remains one of only two players to win Rookie of the Year and MVP in the same season.

prompt: Besides Wes Unseld, which other player won Rookie of the Year and MVP in the same NBA season?
explanation: Wilt Chamberlain accomplished this feat in his rookie season of 1959-60.

prompt: Which team won a record eight consecutive NBA championships from 1959 to 1966?
explanation: The Celtics' eight straight titles from 1959-1966 is an unmatched dynasty in major American sports.

prompt: Which player, drafted by Portland in 1984, is often cited in the debate over the Blazers passing on Michael Jordan?
explanation: Portland selected Sam Bowie second overall in 1984, ahead of Michael Jordan, who went third to Chicago.

prompt: Which team drafted Michael Jordan third overall in the 1984 draft?
explanation: The Chicago Bulls selected Jordan third overall, after Houston (Olajuwon) and Portland (Bowie).

prompt: Which team selected Hakeem Olajuwon first overall in the same 1984 draft as Michael Jordan?
explanation: The Houston Rockets took Hakeem Olajuwon first overall in the star-studded 1984 draft class.

prompt: Which player scored a then career-high 69 points in a single game against Cleveland in 1990?
explanation: Michael Jordan scored 69 points against the Cavaliers in March 1990, his career-high single-game total.

prompt: Which player scored 71 points in his final regular-season game to clinch the scoring title in 1994?
explanation: David Robinson scored 71 points against the Clippers on the final day of the 1993-94 season to win the scoring title.

prompt: Which player scored 100 points on the same night David Robinson pursued the scoring title, essentially forcing Robinson to score more? Who was that rival scorer in 1994?
options: Shaquille O'Neal, who scored 46 points that same day
explanation: Shaquille O'Neal scored 46 points that day, prompting Robinson to chase and surpass the scoring title with his 71-point game.

prompt: Which coach is credited with inventing the 'triangle offense' used by Michael Jordan's Bulls and later the Lakers?
explanation: Tex Winter developed the triangle offense, which Phil Jackson implemented with the Bulls and Lakers.

prompt: Which team traded for Wilt Chamberlain in a multi-player deal in January 1965, bringing him back to the city where he began his career?
explanation: Chamberlain was traded from the San Francisco Warriors to the Philadelphia 76ers in a multi-player deal in January 1965.

prompt: Which team's 2001 championship run, led by Allen Iverson, is remembered for a Finals loss to the Lakers despite a Game 1 win?
explanation: Iverson's 76ers won Game 1 of the 2001 Finals but lost the series 4-1 to the Lakers.

prompt: Which player was named MVP of the 2001 season while leading Philadelphia to the Finals?
explanation: Allen Iverson won the 2001 MVP award, his only one, while leading the 76ers to the Finals.

prompt: Which player famously scored 81 of his team's points, edging closer to but not surpassing Chamberlain's 100-point record, in 2006?
explanation: Kobe Bryant's 81-point game against Toronto in January 2006 is the second-highest scoring game in NBA history.

prompt: Which player scored 13 points in 33 seconds against the Spurs in a memorable comeback in 2004?
explanation: Tracy McGrady's remarkable scoring burst helped Houston complete a comeback win over San Antonio.

prompt: Which player is credited with recording the first officially verified 'quadruple-double' in NBA history, in 1974?
explanation: Nate Thurmond recorded the first officially recognized quadruple-double in October 1974.

prompt: Which team drafted Larry Bird in 1978 but had to wait a year for him to join after finishing college?
explanation: Boston drafted Bird under a special junior-eligible rule in 1978, and he joined the team in 1979.

prompt: Which award did Larry Bird win three consecutive times in the mid-1980s?
explanation: Bird won three straight regular-season MVP awards from 1984 to 1986.

prompt: Which two other players, besides Larry Bird, have won three consecutive MVP awards?
options: Wilt Chamberlain and Larry Bird only, no others
explanation: Wilt Chamberlain and Bill Russell both also won three consecutive MVP awards during the 1960s.

prompt: Which player recorded the most points ever scored by a rookie in a single game, with 58 points, back in 2018?
options: Wilt Chamberlain, as a rookie in 1959
explanation: Wilt Chamberlain's rookie season included a 58-point game in 1959, an extraordinary debut season.

prompt: Which team acquired Moses Malone and won the 1983 NBA championship, sweeping the Lakers?
explanation: Moses Malone led Philadelphia to a title in 1983, famously predicting a 'Fo-Fo-Fo' sweep through the playoffs.

prompt: Which phrase did Moses Malone famously use to predict the 76ers' dominant 1983 playoff run?
explanation: Malone's 'Fo-Fo-Fo' prediction meant sweeping all three playoff rounds; Philadelphia nearly did, losing only one game.

statement: Wilt Chamberlain holds the NBA's all-time career rebounding record.
explanation: Chamberlain's 23,924 career rebounds remain the all-time record.

statement: John Stockton holds both the career assists and career steals records in NBA history.
explanation: Stockton set both records during his long career with the Utah Jazz.

statement: Oscar Robertson averaged a triple-double for an entire NBA season in 1961-62.
explanation: Robertson's historic season went unmatched until Russell Westbrook repeated the feat in 2017.

statement: The Boston Celtics won eight consecutive NBA championships from 1959 to 1966.
explanation: This remains one of the most dominant championship runs in the history of professional sports.

statement: Wilt Chamberlain averaged over 50 points per game across an entire NBA season.
explanation: Chamberlain averaged 50.4 points per game during the 1961-62 season.

statement: David Robinson scored 71 points in a game to win the 1994 scoring title.
explanation: Robinson's 71-point outburst against the Clippers clinched the scoring title on the final day of the season.

statement: Portland drafted Sam Bowie ahead of Michael Jordan in the 1984 NBA draft.
explanation: Portland selected Bowie second overall, one of the most debated draft decisions in NBA history.

statement: Moses Malone predicted a 'Fo-Fo-Fo' sweep for the 76ers' 1983 playoff run.
explanation: Philadelphia nearly delivered on the prediction, losing only one playoff game that postseason.

statement: Bill Russell won a record 11 championships as a player, all with the Boston Celtics.
explanation: Russell's 11 titles in 13 seasons remain unmatched in NBA history.

statement: Hakeem Olajuwon holds the NBA's all-time record for career blocked shots.
explanation: Olajuwon's 3,830 career blocks lead all players in NBA history.

clues: American center
Averaged over 50 points per game in a single season, an NBA record
Also scored 100 points in one game
Rival of Bill Russell throughout the 1960s
explanation: Chamberlain's statistical dominance in the 1960s remains largely unmatched in NBA history.

clues: American guard
Holds the all-time NBA assists and steals records
Long-time Utah Jazz point guard
Formed a legendary pick-and-roll duo with Karl Malone
explanation: Stockton's career assist and steal totals remain NBA records decades after his retirement.

clues: American center
Averaged a triple-double for an entire season in 1961-62
Nicknamed 'The Big O'
Played for the Cincinnati Royals and Milwaukee Bucks
explanation: Robertson's all-around statistical dominance in the early 1960s was historic.

clues: American center
Won 11 championships as a player with Boston
Legendary defensive anchor and rebounder
Rival of Wilt Chamberlain throughout his career
explanation: Russell's 11 titles across 13 seasons make him the most decorated champion in NBA history.

prompt: Order these NBA scoring milestones chronologically (earliest first).
events: Wilt Chamberlain averages 50.4 points for a season (1961-62)
Wilt Chamberlain scores 100 points in a single game (1962)
David Robinson scores 71 points to win the scoring title (1994)
Kobe Bryant scores 81 points in a single game (2006)
explanation: Order: Chamberlain's 50-point average and 100-point game both came in 1962 (average first, then the specific 100-point game), followed by Robinson 1994 and Bryant 2006.

prompt: Order these NBA dynasties by the year their championship run began (earliest first).
events: Boston Celtics begin their eight-year title streak (1959)
Los Angeles Lakers win a then-record 33 straight games (1971-72 season)
Chicago Bulls begin their first three-peat (1991)
San Antonio Spurs win their first title (1999)
explanation: Order: Celtics streak begins 1959, Lakers win streak 1971-72, Bulls first three-peat 1991, Spurs first title 1999.

prompt: Which player scored an NBA-record 4 points in 0.7 seconds using a tip-in and a full-court situation? (Trick question — which two players are famously involved in the '0.7 seconds' Lakers-Kings 2002 controversy?)
explanation: Robert Horry's tip-in with 0.7 seconds left in Game 4 of the 2002 Western Conference Finals against Sacramento is a famous playoff moment.

prompt: Which center's rivalry with Shaquille O'Neal defined the Lakers-Kings playoff battles of the early 2000s?
explanation: Vlade Divac anchored the Sacramento Kings during their fierce playoff rivalry with the Lakers.

prompt: Which player scored a franchise-record 70 points for the Charlotte Hornets/Bobcats franchise history?
explanation: Kemba Walker holds several franchise scoring records for the Charlotte Hornets.

prompt: Which team lost an NBA-record 26 games in a row during the 2010-11 season?
explanation: The Cleveland Cavaliers, in the season after LeBron James left, lost a then-record 26 consecutive games.

prompt: Which team broke the Cavaliers' losing streak record with 28 straight losses in the 2013-14 season?
explanation: The Philadelphia 76ers lost 28 consecutive games during their rebuilding 'Process' era.

prompt: Which term describes the Philadelphia 76ers' intentional rebuilding strategy in the mid-2010s under Sam Hinkie?
explanation: 'Trust the Process' became a rallying cry during Philadelphia's deliberate rebuilding period.

prompt: Which player was the centerpiece draft pick of the 76ers' 'Process' rebuild, taken third overall in 2014 despite a foot injury?
explanation: Embiid missed his first two seasons with foot injuries before becoming an MVP-caliber player.

prompt: Which coach won championships with both the Chicago Bulls and Los Angeles Lakers, totaling 11 titles?
explanation: Phil Jackson's 11 championships across two franchises is an NBA coaching record.

prompt: Which coach won four championships with the Los Angeles Lakers in the 1980s as a player-turned-coach known for 'Showtime'?
explanation: Pat Riley coached the 'Showtime' Lakers to four titles in the 1980s.

prompt: Which team did Pat Riley later coach to a championship in 2006 after leaving the Lakers?
explanation: Riley coached the Miami Heat, with Shaq and Wade, to the 2006 title.

prompt: Which player is credited with the first-ever 'dunk contest' win at the inaugural NBA Slam Dunk Contest in 1984?
explanation: Larry Nance won the first official NBA Slam Dunk Contest held during All-Star Weekend in 1984.

prompt: Which player famously won the Slam Dunk Contest using a free-throw-line takeoff, becoming iconic in dunk contest history?
explanation: Michael Jordan's free-throw-line dunks in the 1987 and 1988 contests became iconic images.

prompt: Which player is widely regarded as having the greatest Slam Dunk Contest performance ever, in 2000, including a dunk over a person?
explanation: Vince Carter's 2000 Slam Dunk Contest performance, including a dunk over 7'2 Frederic Weis, is often cited as the best ever.

prompt: Which player did Vince Carter dunk over during the 2000 Olympics, a moment as famous as his dunk contest win?
explanation: Carter's dunk over French center Frederic Weis at the 2000 Sydney Olympics became known as 'le dunk de la mort' (the dunk of death).

prompt: Which player won back-to-back NBA scoring titles in the late 1990s while playing for Philadelphia, before Iverson's era?
explanation: Allen Iverson won scoring titles in 1999, 2001, 2002 and 2005.

prompt: Which player famously stepped over Tyronn Lue during the 2001 NBA Finals, an iconic and controversial moment?
explanation: Iverson's step-over of Lue after a clutch shot in Game 1 of the 2001 Finals became one of the era's most replayed moments.

prompt: Which team did Wilt Chamberlain briefly play for during a break from the NBA, touring internationally, before returning to set his scoring records?
explanation: Chamberlain played one season with the Harlem Globetrotters in 1958-59 before joining the NBA's Philadelphia Warriors.

prompt: Which player holds the record for most All-Star Game selections in NBA history?
explanation: LeBron James has more All-Star selections than any other player in NBA history.

prompt: Which player holds the record for most All-Star Game MVP awards?
explanation: Kobe Bryant won a record four All-Star Game MVP awards during his career.

prompt: Which team drafted Yao Ming first overall in the 2002 NBA draft?
explanation: Yao Ming, from China, was selected first overall by the Houston Rockets in 2002.

prompt: Which player's career is credited with dramatically growing the NBA's popularity in China during the 2000s?
explanation: Yao Ming's stardom with the Houston Rockets significantly boosted the NBA's popularity in China.

prompt: Which player was the first Chinese-born player to play in the NBA, joining the Dallas Mavericks in 2001?
explanation: Wang Zhizhi became the first Chinese-born NBA player when he joined Dallas in 2001, a year before Yao Ming.

prompt: Which two teams combined for over 370 points in a triple-overtime game in 1983, the highest-scoring NBA game at the time?
options: Golden State Warriors and Denver Nuggets
San Antonio Spurs and Milwaukee Bucks
explanation: The Pistons beat the Nuggets 186-184 in triple overtime in 1983, the highest-scoring game in NBA history at the time.

prompt: What was the final score of the 1983 triple-overtime game between Detroit and Denver, the highest-scoring game in NBA history at the time?
explanation: Detroit won 186-184 in a game that still ranks among the highest-scoring in NBA history.

prompt: Which team won an NBA-record 116-point margin of victory in a single game in 1991?
explanation: The Cavaliers beat the Miami Heat 148-80, a 68-point margin — however the true record-margin game (149-83, a 68-point win) was Cleveland over Miami in December 1991.

prompt: Which two teams were involved in the largest margin-of-victory game in NBA history?
options: Los Angeles Lakers over Golden State Warriors
Boston Celtics over New York Knicks
explanation: The Cavaliers beat the Heat by a record 68 points, 148-80, in December 1991.

statement: Robert Horry's famous tip-in shot with 0.7 seconds left came against the Sacramento Kings in the 2002 playoffs.
explanation: Horry's shot in Game 4 of the Western Conference Finals is one of the most memorable playoff moments of the era.

statement: The Philadelphia 76ers set the NBA record for consecutive losses during their 'Process' rebuilding era.
explanation: Philadelphia lost 28 straight games during the 2013-14 season.

statement: Vince Carter's 2000 Slam Dunk Contest is widely regarded as one of the greatest dunk contest performances in NBA history.
explanation: His dunk over Frederic Weis at that year's Olympics further cemented his dunking legacy.

statement: Yao Ming was the first Chinese-born player to appear in the NBA.
explanation: Wang Zhizhi preceded Yao Ming, joining the Dallas Mavericks in 2001, a year before Yao's debut.

statement: The Cleveland Cavaliers hold the record for the largest margin of victory in a single NBA game.
explanation: Cleveland beat Miami 148-80 in December 1991, a 68-point margin.

statement: Detroit and Denver combined for over 370 points in a triple-overtime game in 1983.
explanation: The Pistons won 186-184 in a game still remembered for its extraordinary scoring.

statement: Phil Jackson won NBA championships as a head coach with three different franchises.
explanation: Jackson won championships with only two franchises: the Chicago Bulls and Los Angeles Lakers.

statement: Allen Iverson stepped over Tyronn Lue during Game 1 of the 2001 NBA Finals.
explanation: This moment remains one of the most iconic and replayed clips from that Finals series.

statement: LeBron James holds the record for most NBA All-Star selections.
explanation: LeBron has been selected to more All-Star Games than any player in league history.

statement: Kobe Bryant won a record four NBA All-Star Game MVP awards.
explanation: Kobe's four All-Star MVP awards are the most of any player.

clues: American forward/center
Made a famous game-clinching tip-in shot with 0.7 seconds left in 2002
Known as a clutch playoff performer despite modest regular-season stats
Won championships with Houston, the Lakers, and the Spurs
explanation: Horry earned the nickname 'Big Shot Rob' for his clutch playoff shot-making across multiple championship teams.

clues: Chinese center
First Chinese-born player to appear in the NBA
Joined the Dallas Mavericks in 2001
Preceded Yao Ming's NBA debut by a year
explanation: Wang Zhizhi broke ground for Chinese players in the NBA before Yao Ming's stardom.

Nicknamed 'The Answer'
Won the 2001 MVP award leading Philadelphia to the Finals
Famous for stepping over Tyronn Lue in the 2001 Finals
explanation: Iverson's crossover and scoring ability made him one of the most electrifying players of his era.

Won the 2000 Slam Dunk Contest, considered by many the greatest ever
Famously dunked over a 7'2 French center at the Sydney Olympics
Nicknamed 'Vinsanity'
explanation: Carter's dunking exploits in 2000 remain legendary in NBA highlight reels.

prompt: Order these NBA rebuilding and draft milestones chronologically (earliest first).
events: Yao Ming is drafted first overall by Houston (2002)
The Philadelphia 76ers begin 'The Process' under Sam Hinkie (2013)
The 76ers lose a record 28 straight games (2013-14 season)
Joel Embiid wins his first MVP award (2023)
explanation: Order: Yao Ming draft 2002, Process begins 2013, 28-game losing streak 2013-14, Embiid MVP 2023.

prompt: Order these NBA head coaching championship achievements chronologically by when the coach's first title came (earliest first).
events: Red Auerbach wins his first title with Boston (1957)
Pat Riley wins his first title with the Lakers (1982)
Phil Jackson wins his first title with the Bulls (1991)
Gregg Popovich wins his first title with the Spurs (1999)
explanation: Order: Auerbach 1957, Riley 1982, Jackson 1991, Popovich 1999.

prompt: Which player's jersey number 6 was retired league-wide by the NBA in 2022, an unprecedented honor?
explanation: The NBA retired Bill Russell's number 6 across the entire league in 2022, the first such honor in league history.
